channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Nobody reads your test reports. Here's how I re-engineered them with a 3-layer architecture
item.description: Note: BDR (Behavior-Driven Living Requirements) is my own architectural approach to organizing Playwright tests — a Cucumber-free alternative to BDD that I designed and documented at...

item.title: Building a Production LLM Evaluation Harness in Pytest: Cost-Bounded, Flake-Aware, CI-Gated (Runnable Python)
item.description: I shipped my fourth LLM agent to production last quarter. By month two, the eval suite that "passed in CI" was the reason a regression made it to a customer. The tests were green. But they were...

item.title: What is physical AI - is it more than just hype?
item.description: Physical AI has become a real trend over the past year. At CES (Consumer Electronics Show), NVIDIA's Jensen Huang declared 2026 the "ChatGPT moment for physical AI." Georgetown's Center for...

item.title: PYTHON IN DATA ANALYSIS
item.description: Introduction to Python for Data Analytics What is Data Analytics? Data analytics is the process of collecting, cleaning, analyzing, and interpreting data to uncover meaningful insights and support...

item.title: Bird Meadow: a multi-agent Active Inference world I'd like the community to poke holes in
item.description: TL;DR. I'm Michael Polzin. I just shipped, as open source, a multi-agent Active Inference world — birds that hear and sing — running on top of audit-corrected variational free energy / expected...

item.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: Which No-Code Database in 2026: Tested & Compared
item.description: In 2025, 68% of startups reported wasting $14k+ on no-code database tools that couldn’t scale past 10k concurrent users, according to the 2026 State of No-Code Engineering report. After...

item.title: Build a voice agent that can make outbound calls with AssemblyAI
item.description: Why outbound voice agents matter A voice agent that can dial out, not just answer, unlocks workflows that text channels drop the ball on: Use case What the agent does Why outbound beats inbound...

item.title: Build a voice agent with LiveKit and AssemblyAI’s Voice Agent API
item.description: Why combine LiveKit and the Voice Agent API WebRTC and AI are different problems with different best-in-class solutions: LiveKit is the easiest way to ship production-grade real-time audio. SDKs...

item.title: We Built a Poor Man’s o1 on AWS for $0.25 – And You Can Too
item.description: I remember the first time I tried OpenAI’s o1. I asked it a gnarly infrastructure question: “Design a multi‑region, strongly consistent queue that survives a full AWS region outage.” It paused for...

item.title: Did Microsoft just tease a new Xbox UI?
item.description: Microsoft showed off a "consistent" Xbox UI across handhelds, consoles, and cloud gaming during its Xbox keynote at the Game Developers Conference in March. At the time it was difficult to see if...

item.title: Open-source licenses 101: which one to actually pick
item.description: Sooner or later, every developer runs into The License Question. You shipped something to GitHub, GitHub asked you to pick a license, and you scrolled the dropdown — MIT, Apache, GPL, AGPL, BUSL,...

item.title: ChatGPT Has 'Goblin' Mania in the US. In China It Will 'Catch You Steadily'
item.description: OpenAI's chatbot has some weird linguistic tics in Chinese that are driving users crazy.

item.title: How Anthropic’s Mythos has rewritten Firefox’s approach to cybersecurity
item.description: Security researchers at Mozilla say Anthropic's Mythos has unearthed a wealth of high-severity bugs in Firefox.

item.title: A hacker ran me over with a robot lawn mower
item.description: I'm lying in the dirt. It's coming for me. Then, with a lurch, it's climbing up my chest. If Andreas Makris doesn't stop the 200-pound robot lawn mower in time, it could drag its blades across my...

item.title: Inside the return of Xbox
item.description: Two weeks ago there was a buzz in the air inside Microsoft's studio D building. Hundreds of Xbox employees gathered early on a Thursday morning, packed into the hallways and atrium, to hear from...

item.title: The future of Disney Plus is a confused mess
item.description: Newly minted CEO Josh D'Amaro says that he wants to turn Disney Plus into "the immersive, interactive digital centerpiece of the company." It used to be that people went to the movies or theme...

item.title: There’s actually a good deal happening now on the Xbox Series X
item.description: It’s been tough to recommend the Xbox Series X recently, especially at the $649 price it has sold at since last October. But if you’re thinking about getting one, at least consider the Forza...

item.title: Netflix has its own, impressive AI-powered voice search
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. A small subset of Netflix...

item.title: BMW iX3 has a lower starting price than comparable gas-powered X3
item.description: The 2027 BMW iX3 is now available in the US, and its starting price my surprise and delight fans of the Bavarian automaker. The iX3, which is the first vehicle built on BMW's next-gen Neue Klasse...

item.title: Apple’s $599 MacBook Neo could be at risk from rising RAM prices
item.description: The MacBook Neo might lose its most appealing trait thanks to the ongoing RAM shortage. According to analyst Tim Culpan, Apple could discontinue the budget-priced $599 base configuration of the...

item.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27: A shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K
item.description: Startup Battlefield 200 applications are open, but only for three more weeks. Apply by May 27 for your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, $100,000 equity-free, and more...

item.title: Samsung’s flagship laptop is a MacBook Pro clone gone horribly wrong
item.description: II know I'm not the only person who wants a MacBook Pro for Windows: a sleek, ultra powerful, super portable machine with an excellent screen and high-quality build. Samsung's Galaxy Book6 Ultra...

item.title: Exhibit at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Get in front of 10,000 decision-makers before space runs out
item.description: If startup visibility, traction, and real deals matter, you should be on the TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 exhibit floor. Get your 6' exhibit table before your competitor does.

item.title: Aurora’s Chris Urmson on why self-driving trucks are finally ready to scale
item.description: Self-driving has been “almost here” for over a decade. But somewhere between DARPA challenges and a handful of driverless trucks hauling freight between Dallas and Houston, Aurora co-founder and...

item.title: 2 days left: Get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.description: Two days left to save up to $410 on your pass, and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Offer ends May 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now.

item.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
item.description: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient...

item.title: China’s Moonshot AI raises $2B at $20B valuation as demand for open source AI skyrockets
item.description: Moonshot's annualized recurring revenue topped $200 million in April, driven by rapid growth in paid subscriptions and API usage.

item.title: PAN-OS RCE Exploit Under Active Use Enabling Root Access and Espionage
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has disclosed that threat actors may have attempted to unsuccessfully exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw as early as April 9, 2026. The vulnerability in...

item.title: Spotify wants to become the home for AI-generated personal audio
item.description: Users will be able to create a podcast from Codex or Claude Code and import it to Spotify.
